name: Cilantro Coleslaw
directions: Mix dressing ingredients and toss with veggies.
Serve.

name: Super Cole Slaw
directions: Whisk first 7 ingredients in a snall bowl to blend.
Combine all remaining ingredients in a large bowl.
Add dressing, then toss to coat well.
Dressing may be made one day in advance, but let sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before mixing with freshly prepared salad.
Sprinkle with chopped peanuts as garnish if desired.

name: Easy Guacamole
directions: Combine all ingredients in the bowl of a food processor.
Pulse until coarsely chopped, then puree until desired consistancy is reached.

name: Easy Gazpacho
directions: Mix together and chill.

name: Asian Cole Slaw
directions: Place arame in a medium bowl and fill with enough cold water to cover.  Let soak 20 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine cabbage, kale and carrots.
Make dressing: in a small bowl, combine all the dressing ingredients and whisk until well blended.
Drain and rinse arame.  Add areme to cabbage mixture, pour over dressing and toss to mix.
Let stand at room temperature at least 30 minutes before serving.

name: Cole Slaw  (Vinegar Based)
directions: In large bowl with lid mix slaw mix, onion and celery seeds.
Pour splenda over salad.
Mix water and vinegar and pour over salad.
Refrigerate overnight stirring periodically.
You can also put in large zipper bag and turn instead of stirring.

name: Crunchy Peanut Slaw
directions: Shred the cabbage very finely. The fineness of the shredded cabbage is really what makes this salad; you want it in in threads, almost, and with the threads chopped into bite-size lengths.
Toss with the peanuts in a large bowl.
Chop the green onions, including the green and white parts.
Toss the onions and chopped cilantro with the cabbage, seasoning very lightly with salt and pepper.
Whisk the dressing until emulsified, then taste and adjust to your own preferences of sweetness and saltiness.
Toss with the cabbage. Garnish with a few more peanuts and serve.

name: Cole Slaw, Vegan
directions: Slice the cabbage as finely as you have patience for, and do the same with the cucumber.
Place all the above in a large bowl, add water to just cover.  Keep in fridge, and mix  occasionally.
After 3 or 4 hours, drain off most the liquid through a fine mesh colander (to keep most of the seeds).   Serve.

name: Mexican Coleslaw
directions: Mix first 4 ingredients in a small bowl until sugar dissolves. Pour the dressing over the cabbage, toss well and refrigerate.
